introSection: Bocaraca is a steel roller coaster located at Parque Diversiones in La Uruca, San José, Costa Rica.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The roller coaster first opened in 1984 at Playland Park in New York as Whirlwind, alongside the Wild Cat. It operated until 1992 when it was dismantled. The roller coaster opened at Knoebels Amusement Resort in 1993, replacing Jet Star which closed the previous year. It closed in 2004 and was sold to Parque Diversiones in Costa Rica. The space where Whirlwind stood is now occupied by Flying Turns. The attraction reopened as Bocaraca in 2005.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: This is a relatively short roller coaster with a curved first drop and two separate corkscrew inversions. This roller coaster has a compact footprint, with a bend in the middle of the station.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Yellow track and blue supports. The ride used to feature white track and green supports when in the USA.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train was built by Arrow Dynamics and has over-the-shoulder harnesses.

fullText:
Bocaraca is a steel roller coaster located at Parque Diversiones in La Uruca, San José, Costa Rica.

The roller coaster first opened in 1984 at Playland Park in New York as Whirlwind, alongside the Wild Cat. It operated until 1992 when it was dismantled. The roller coaster opened at Knoebels Amusement Resort in 1993, replacing Jet Star which closed the previous year. It closed in 2004 and was sold to Parque Diversiones in Costa Rica. The space where Whirlwind stood is now occupied by Flying Turns. The attraction reopened as Bocaraca in 2005.

This is a relatively short roller coaster with a curved first drop and two separate corkscrew inversions. This roller coaster has a compact footprint, with a bend in the middle of the station.

Yellow track and blue supports. The ride used to feature white track and green supports when in the USA.

The train was built by Arrow Dynamics and has over-the-shoulder harnesses.
